content: We can reframe Self-Refine's single-stream iterative self-feedback framework as a base that can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or supervision while fixing its limitation of uniform single-perspective feedback bias. This hybrid framework would enable LLMs to not only iteratively refine their outputs, but also challenge their own initial assumptions from competing angles to reach more robust results without additional data or training overhead.
